source_jobs.title: Technical Escalations Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About the Team:

Born at X, Google's Moonshot Factory, Taara is on a mission to connect billions of people lacking abundant and affordable internet today by pioneering the way we use light to deliver faster, cheaper, more reliable connectivity. You can help lead the charge in bringing our groundbreaking wireless optical communication and photonics chip technologies to the world. Join us to light the way for bridging the digital divide and illuminating the future.

About the Role:

At Taara, we are looking for a skilled technical problem solver to join our team. Technical Escalations Engineer will be responsible for replicating complex field issues, performing deep-dive root cause analysis on wireless optical communication (WOC) products, and driving strong alignment and shared understanding among our customer support, network operations, product and engineering teams. You’ll also be a primary owner of our user acceptance testing work streams, helping to ensure that our hardware and software enhancements will work seamlessly in the array of network environments in which our customers deploy our products.

How you will make 10x impact:

 - Issue Replication & RCA: Build lab environments to replicate customer-reported performance issues. Perform root cause analysis (RCA) on hardware and networking failures in close partnership with the Engineering lead.

 - Knowledge Engineering: Translate your findings into actionable intelligence. You’ll draft troubleshooting guides, Method of Procedures (MOPs), and internal resources to level up our frontline support teams.

 - Advanced Troubleshooting: Act as the Tier 3 escalation point for complex technical issues that require more than just a reboot.

 - Quality Assurance & UAT: Assist with User Acceptance Testing (UAT) by performing QA in diverse customer environments and various network configurations to ensure stability before full rollouts.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work directly with Product, Network Operations, and Engineering to influence product improvements and prioritization based on observed field data and recurring issues.

What you should have:

 - Experience: At least 5 years of experience in technical solutions engineering, test engineering, or a similar role.

 - Networking Guru: Deep understanding of network protocols, topology, and hardware performance.

 - The "Hacker" Mindset: You enjoy breaking things just to figure out how to fix them. You are persistent in finding the "why" behind a bug.

 - Technical Writing: Ability to take a complex technical solution and document it into a clear, step-by-step guide for others.

 - Collaboration: Comfortable working in the trenches with Engineering while still being able to communicate effectively with customer-facing teams.

 - Ability to work in the Sunnyvale office at least 3 days per week.
   
   

It would be great if you also had these:

 - Direct experience with free space optics and/or radio frequency hardware



The US base salary range for this full-time position is $165,000-185,000 + bonus + equity + benefits. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your location during the hiring process.

source_jobs.title: Systems Supply Chain Manager
source_jobs.description:
About the Team:

Born at X, Google's Moonshot Factory, Taara is on a mission to connect billions of people lacking abundant and affordable internet today by pioneering the way we use light to deliver faster, cheaper, more reliable connectivity. Lead the charge in bringing our groundbreaking wireless optical communication and photonics chip technologies to the world. Drive our growth story as we scale innovative solutions across the world. Join us to light the way for bridging the digital divide and illuminating the future.



About the Role:



Taara is looking to hire a results-oriented Systems Supply Chain Manager with a specialized focus on optimizing and managing complex supply chains, ideally possessing deep expertise in optics and/or the photonics industry. The successful candidate will demonstrate a verifiable track record of successful supplier performance through strategic on-time delivery initiatives, aggressive cost reduction programs, and supplier relationship management.



How you will make 10x impact:



 - As a Systems Supply Chain Manager, you will report to a senior member of management and play a pivotal role in driving organizational alignment, facilitating communication, and ensuring operational efficiency across all functions of the company.

 - Instrumental in coordinating reliability, quality and material planning across the entire supply chain . This includes driving Design for Manufacturing (DFM) initiatives to optimize product buildability and leading Failure Modes and Effects Analysis (FMEA) activities for cycle time improvement.

 - Own supply commitments for multiple product groups (Optics, PCBAs, Mechanicals), and drive resolution of supply issues, creating should costs reporting, and working across manufacturing and engineering groups.

 - Work closely with contract manufacturing partners and managing vendors to remove obstacles, provide resources, and manage costs to meet build plans and align on favorable supply plans.

 - Implement Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) methodologies across the supply chain to optimize expenditures from raw material acquisition through final product delivery, ensuring predefined cost-down targets are met.

 - Coordinate and facilitate executive meetings, including agenda setting, documentation, and follow-up on action items.



What you should have:



 - Bachelor's degree in Industrial Engineering or Supply Chain Management or equivalent, and 10+ years of supply chain management with complex product experience from industries such as semiconductor, telecommunications, networking, hardware manufacturing, optics, photonics, automotives, aerospace, AR/VR/MR etc.

 - 4+ years of knowledge or experience in NPI including developing project schedules, managing budgets, contributing in supplier selection, and product build management during proto-EVT-DVT-PVT phases.

 - Knowledge of supply chain strategies (e.g., inventory reduction plans, supplier consolidation, lead time reductions, hedging, warehouse inventory management).

 - Experience with MRP systems (Material Resource Planning) for example SAP MRP, Oracle NetSuite or equivalent and good understanding of PLM systems.

 - Identify build requirements in comparison to suppliers capacity (e.g. fixed install capacity, realistic capacity) and consider supplier elements or other considerations

 - Good understanding of bill-of-materials (BOM) structures, configuration management, clear-to-build process, engineering change order (ECO) process, and the product life cycle.

 - Ability to champion technical solutions to manufacturability issues found with the design and collaborate effectively with peers, interdisciplinary engineering groups and vendors.

 - Excellent communicator with strong problem-solving, analytical, and collaboration skills, able to influence and negotiate effectively with both internal and external stakeholders.

 - Oversee production schedules considering terminal builds, priorities, materials availabilit

source_jobs.title: FPGA Design Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Taara Connect:

Born at X, Google's Moonshot Factory, Taara is on a mission to connect billions of people lacking abundant and affordable internet today by pioneering the way we use light to deliver faster, cheaper, more reliable connectivity. Lead the charge in bringing our groundbreaking wireless optical communication and photonics chip technologies to the world. Drive our growth story as we scale innovative solutions across the world. Join us to light the way for bridging the digital divide and illuminating the future.

Role Overview:

We are seeking a highly-motivated individual that enjoys working in a small dynamic team environment with a passion for solving challenging problems that can lead to high-impact advances in technology. As an FPGA Design Engineer, you will be responsible for the full lifecycle of FPGA designs – design, simulation, implementation, testing, and verification – for digital logic for high-data-rate communications systems and precision line-of-sight tracking systems.

About the Role:

 - Collaborate across teams to jointly develop high-speed digital signal processing and control system algorithms supporting wireless optical communications applications.

 - Implement custom RTL modules that enable high data rate communications, as well as timing-sensitive feedback-based precision line-of-sight tracking systems.

 - Develop test benches for RTL modules, perform simulation, and verify design requirements are met.

 - Integrate third party IP cores into an FPGA system, create custom RTL wrappers for third party cores, and interface with IP vendors.

 - Participate in board bring-up activities, system level integration tasks, troubleshooting, and field testing.

 - Stay current with the latest FPGA technologies, tools, and best practices.

 - Contribute ideas and participate in improving our design process, infrastructure, and products.

What you should have:

 - Bachelor of Science in Computer Engineering or similar discipline.

 - 3+ years in FPGA/ Digital Logic Design role, with experience in implementing and testing custom RTL using System Verilog or Verilog.

 - Experience with FPGA design flow including simulation, synthesis, and static timing analysis.

 - Experience with FPGA high speed interfaces.

 - Hands on experience with lab equipment including oscilloscopes and hardware debugging.

 - Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills.

 - Strong communication and teamwork skills.



It would be great if you also had these:

 - MS (or higher) in Computer Engineering, or a related field.

 - Experience with AMD UltraScale/UltraScale+ FPGAs/SoCs, or equivalent.

 - Experience coding in embedded C/C++ (or similar) and common scripting languages (e.g. Python, tcl).

 - Experience implementing RTL blocks for high-speed communications, image processing, and real-time tracking systems.

 - Experience with Simulink or High Level Synthesis.

The US base salary range for this full-time position is $160,000-$210,000 + bonus + equity + benefits. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your location during the hiring process.

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
About the Team:

Born at X, Google's Moonshot Factory, Taara is on a mission to connect billions of people lacking abundant and affordable internet today by pioneering the way we use light to deliver faster, cheaper, more reliable connectivity. You can help lead the charge in bringing our groundbreaking wireless optical communication and photonics chip technologies to the world. Join us to light the way for bridging the digital divide and illuminating the future.

About the Role:

At Taara, we succeed when our customers succeed. We are looking for a proactive, organized, and empathetic Customer Success Manager to serve as a primary relationship manager for our existing customers. This is a role for a self-starter who excels in a dynamic environment, building processes from the ground up to support groundbreaking technology. You will be the bridge between our customers and our product, helping us to better understand our customers and their needs. You’ll be an internal champion for our customers, delivering crisp insights about our strengths, friction points, and opportunities for improvement.

How you will make 10x impact:

 - Relationship Management: Act as the dedicated point of contact for a portfolio of global customers in the internet connectivity and telecom space, fostering deep trust and ensuring high levels of satisfaction.

 - Strategic Advocacy: Understand customer needs & business goals and provide tailored recommendations to help them maximize the value of our products.

 - Customer Satisfaction and Retention: Monitor account health, identify potential churn risks, and increase visibility into the customer experience.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with Sales, Product, and Support teams to relay customer feedback that drive operational improvements & help to shape the product roadmap.

 - Process Development & Navigating Ambiguity: Build new customer engagement best practices from the ground up and help to develop scalable frameworks that align with our growth.

What you should have:

 - Experience: At least 2 years of experience in Customer Success, Account Management, or a high-touch client-facing role.

 - Communication Mastery: You communicate clearly and can engage with diverse stakeholders. You can explain complex ideas simply and are just as comfortable giving a presentation as you are making a phone call or writing a concise email.

 - Organizational Prowess: You can juggle multiple priorities without dropping the ball and are diligent in documenting and tracking your work.

 - Empathy: You are an excellent listener and genuinely care about helping others, and you can navigate difficult conversations with grace and professionalism.

 - Ability to work in the Sunnyvale office at least 3 days per week.

It would be great if you also had these:

 - Familiarity with and experience in the telecommunications industry

 - Experience with CRM tools, such as Salesforce or ServiceNow



The US base salary range for this full-time position is $125,000-$140,000 + bonus + equity + benefits. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your location during the hiring process.

source_jobs.title: Board Design Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About the Team:

Born at X, Google's Moonshot Factory, Taara is on a mission to connect billions of people lacking abundant and affordable internet today by pioneering the way we use light to deliver faster, cheaper, more reliable connectivity. Lead the charge in bringing our groundbreaking wireless optical communication and photonics chip technologies to the world. Drive our growth story as we scale innovative solutions across the world. Join us to light the way for bridging the digital divide and illuminating the future.

About the Role:

Taara is seeking a Board Design Engineer to lead the complete printed circuit board design from concept to volume product.  This individual will be responsible for the architectural design, schematic capture, layout, fabrication, test and qualification of complex high speed printed circuit boards (PCBs).  The candidate will be proficient in high-speed digital design, signal integrity, power integrity, wirebonding and board fabrication. They’ll work closely across teams including photonics integrated circuits, electronic integrated circuit, mechanical design and software in developing the board designs.

The position seeks highly-motivated individuals that enjoy working in a small dynamic team environment, with a passion for solving challenging problems that can lead to high-impact advances in technology.

How you’ll make 10x impact

 - Designing the analog and digital circuitry including high speed transceivers, power, memory, and sensitive receivers

 - Selecting electrical component including SoCs, FPGAs and high speed switches optimizing for performance and cost

 - Ensuring signal integrity and power integrity through modeling and lab characterization

 - Managing the PCB Layout including detailed signal and power integrity electrical constraint documentation

 - Managing component vendors and contract manufacturers for fabrication and testing including defining and coordination with test software resources

 - Leveraging system knowledge including EMI/EMC, reliability and compliance 

What you should have:

 - Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering and 5+ years of related experience

 - Familiar with Product Lifecycle Management tools and ECO management

 - In-depth knowledge of manufacturing and process tools 

 - Ability to work in the Sunnyvale office at least 3 days per week.

It would be great if you also had these:

 - Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering, or a related field

 - Knowledge of semiconductor design, measurement and packaging

 - Experience in analog and mixed-signal IC design using Cadence Virtuoso and Siemens/Mentor Calibre for DRC/LVS verification

 - Experience in organic or silicon interposer design for advanced packaging

The US base salary range for this full-time position is $160,000 - $210,000 + bonus + equity + benefits. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your location during the hiring process.

source_jobs.title: Head of Marketing
source_jobs.description:
About the Team:

Born at X, Google's Moonshot Factory, Taara is on a mission to connect billions of people lacking abundant and affordable internet today by pioneering the way we use light to deliver faster, cheaper, more reliable connectivity. Lead the charge in bringing our groundbreaking wireless optical communication and photonics chip technologies to the world. Drive our growth story as we scale innovative solutions across the world. Join us to light the way for bridging the digital divide and illuminating the future.

About the Role:

As the Head of Marketing, you will be responsible for developing and executing a comprehensive global marketing and communications strategy that elevates our brand, builds community, and supports the business growth with our partners. You will work closely with cross-functional team members across product, engineering, business development, and operations. This is a unique opportunity to shape the narrative of a transformative technology with significant social impact.

This role will be based in Sunnyvale, California with occasional global travel.

How you will make 10X impact:

 - Marketing & Brand Strategy: Develop and execute a comprehensive global and communications strategy aligned with Taara’s vision, mission, and commercial objectives, in alignment with milestones, timelines, and resource requirements. Evolve target audiences, positioning, messaging, and brand identity.

 - Product Marketing: Partner with the sales team to shape the go-to-market strategy for new and existing products, including market segmentation, value proposition development, messaging, sales enablement materials, and launch plans

 - Demand Generation & Customer Acquisition at Scale: Develop and implement integrated marketing campaigns across digital, content, events, and partner channels to build pipeline, generate qualified leads, and support sales targets, primarily focusing on B2B customer acquisition (ISPs, telcos, resellers). Execute targeted marketing campaigns to specific customer segments, and optimize the customer journey and conversion funnel to maximize acquisition and retention.

 - Communications & Public Relations: Build and manage Taara's external communications strategy, including public relations, analyst relations, social media presence, and thought leadership initiatives to elevate brand visibility and credibility globally.

 - Content Creation: Generate high-quality content (case studies, white papers, sales enablement tools, thought leadership, blog posts, videos, presentations, etc.) that effectively communicates Taara’s value position and impact to technical and business audiences

 - Market Intelligence: Monitor market trends, competitive landscape, and customer insights to inform strategy, identify new opportunities, and ensure Taara maintains a competitive edge.

 - Budget Management: Develop and manage the marketing budget effectively, ensuring optimal allocation of resources and tracking ROI.

 - Collaboration: Work closely with Sales, Product, Engineering, and Leadership teams to ensure alignment and drive collective success.

What you should have: 

 - 10+ years of progressive marketing leadership experience, with a significant portion in B2B technology environments.

 - Proven track record of developing and executing successful global marketing and communications strategies that drive brand awareness, lead generation, and revenue growth.

 - Demonstrated experience in product marketing for hardware or complex technical products.

 - Strong understanding of the full marketing mix, including digital marketing, content marketing, PR, analyst relations, and events.

 - Exceptional communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to articulate complex technical concepts to diverse audiences.  

 - Data-driven mindset with strong analytical skills and experience using metrics to inform decisions and measure success.

 - Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Communicatio

source_jobs.title: Linux System Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About the Team:

Born at X, Google's Moonshot Factory, Taara is on a mission to connect billions of people lacking abundant and affordable internet today by pioneering the way we use light to deliver faster, cheaper, more reliable connectivity. Lead the charge in bringing our groundbreaking wireless optical communication and photonics chip technologies to the world. Drive our growth story as we scale innovative solutions across the world. Join us to light the way for bridging the digital divide and illuminating the future.

About the Role:

Taara is looking to hire a Linux System Software Engineer to build the next generation operating system for wireless optical communication networks.

The ideal candidate will have strong experience developing system-level software in a Linux environment, multi-threaded programming techniques, state-machine and event-driven programming, integration with cloud-based back-end systems, data structures, algorithms, and software development in C++ for both development and unit/regression testing

How you will make 10x impact:

 - Design, develop, and maintain system-level software applications for our next generation wireless optical terminal

 - Build expertise in Taara’s unique silicon photonics and active steering technology

 - Collaborate with engineers and operators to implement telemetry/monitoring solutions

 - Use scalable reliability engineering practices to troubleshoot and resolve issues in large-scale field deployments

 - Stay up-to-date on the latest IoT device trends/technologies

What you should have:

 - Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Networking, Electrical Engineering, or a related field

 - 5+ years of experience in developing application software for Linux-based platforms

 - Experience in multi-threaded programming techniques, state-machine and event-driven programming

 - Strong programming skills in C++ for both development and unit/regression testing

 - Strong understanding of data structures and algorithms

 - Experience interacting with hardware peripherals (SPI, I2C, UART, etc)

 - Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills

 - Strong communication and teamwork skills

 - Ability to work in the Sunnyvale office at least 3 days per week

It would be great if you also had these:

 - Experience with Golang and/or Python

 - Experience with streaming telemetry / network management protocols (gRPC, OpenTelemetry, SNMP etc)

 - Experience with embedded Linux internals: U-boot, secure boot, kernel driver development

 - Understanding of modern security best practices

 - Relevant background in telecommunication or networking industries

 - Experience working in start-up like environments where things can change on a dime

 - Joy in learning new technologies, learning from your teammates and teaching them as well

The US base salary range for this full-time position is $160,000 - $210,000 + bonus + equity + benefits. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your location during the hiring process.

source_jobs.title: Senior Photonic Circuits Design Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About the Team

Born at X, Google's Moonshot Factory, Taara is on a mission to connect billions of people who lack abundant and affordable internet by pioneering high-throughput, long-range free-space optical communication (FSOC). Our links use eye-safe lasers propagating between two terminals to transmit high-speed data wirelessly. Lead the charge in bringing our groundbreaking wireless optical communication to the world. Join us to light the way toward bridging the digital divide and illuminating the future.

About the Role

Taara is seeking a Senior Photonic Circuits Design Engineer to work on next-generation photonic and electronic integrated modules as part of the R&D team developing cutting-edge wireless optical communication systems based on integrated optical phased arrays.

The successful candidate will own photonic circuit architecture, photonic spectral filter design, and photonic switch design within the very-large-scale photonic integrated circuits (PICs) designed at Taara. This includes contributing to photonic layout and to system considerations such as optical link modeling. The role requires extensive experience in photonic design and layout—including chip-level ownership of photonic circuit design for commercial foundry tapeouts—and deep expertise in spectral filter design and communication link budgets.

We are looking for a highly motivated individual who thrives in ambiguity, enjoys working in a small, dynamic team environment, and has a passion for solving challenging problems that lead to high-impact technological advances.

How you will make 10x impact:

 - Own and deliver record-breaking photonic integrated circuit designs.

 - Contribute to photonic layout, system-level optimization, and product strategy.

 - Demonstrate a strong motivation to make the world a better place through technology and a desire to be part of the team making this happen.

What you should have:

 - Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering, Applied Physics, Materials Science, or a related field.

 - Five (5) or more years of relevant industry experience with demonstrated achievements in photonic circuit designs.

 - Extensive expertise in photonic spectral filter design and photonic link modeling.

 - Expertise in photonic switch design, antenna theory, and photonic layout.

 - Experience with photonic device simulation software (Lumerical or equivalent).

 - Experience with optical link simulation software (VPIphotonics, Lumerical Interconnect, or equivalent).

 - Hands-on experience with multiple tapeouts at commercial foundries.

It would be great if you also had these:

 - Experience with Python, Git, and gdsfactory.

 - Experience with Design Rule Checks (DRC) and Layout Versus Schematic (LVS).

 - Experience with commercial layout verification software (Calibre or equivalent).

 - Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to work effectively with senior external partners.

 - Hands-on experience in advancing a product from the prototype phase to the final production stage.

Hybrid/Onsite in Sunnyvale, CA is preferred. Relocation assistance can be provided.

The US base salary range for this full-time position is $220,000 - $270,000 (inclusive of bonus at target) + equity + benefits. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your location during the hiring process.

source_jobs.title: Product Manager: Data Centers
source_jobs.description:
About the Team:

Taara extends and amplifies global communications infrastructure using beams of light to deliver high-speed connectivity. Born at X, Google’s moonshot factory, Taara works with telecommunications providers, partners, and internal teams to plan, deploy, and operate optical wireless networks around the world.

As Taara scales deployments and evolves its products, we are looking for a Data Center Product Manager to define and evolve products that enable high-capacity, reliable connectivity within and between data centers and network facilities.

About the Role:

This role, Product Manager: Data Centers, focuses on shaping Taara’s products for data center and infrastructure use cases. You will work closely with engineering, UX design, and customer-facing teams to translate real-world networking infrastructure needs into clear product requirements. Your work will ensure Taara’s connectivity solutions meet the performance, availability, and scalability expectations of data center environments. This work is suited for someone comfortable working with data center topologies, multiple connectivity needs in data centers - rack to rack, intrarack, building to building, and data center to point of presence, network constraints, and complex deployment environments.

How you will make 10x impact:

 - Own the product definition and evolution of products supporting data center and infrastructure connectivity use cases.

 - Translate customer, partner, and internal needs into clear product requirements, workflows, and roadmaps.

 - Define product requirements related to capacity, latency, availability, redundancy, scalability, and operational constraints.

 - Partner closely with engineering to balance technical feasibility, product performance, and deployment realities.

 - Define and track key product metrics related to performance, availability, adoption, and operational effectiveness.

 - Incorporate learnings from deployments and live environments into ongoing iteration and continuous improvement.Taara’s data center and infrastructure products meet enterprise expectations for availability, performance, and scalability.

 - Product requirements are clear and actionable, enabling engineering teams to build with confidence and minimal rework.

 - Deployments integrate smoothly into existing data center and network environments.



What you should have:

 - 5+ years of experience in product management for infrastructure, networking, or technical systems.

 - 2+ years in data center environment

 - 1+ years of experience building 0-1 products

 - Experience translating complex technical needs into clear product requirements and priorities.

 - Understanding connectivity needs within data center environments, network infrastructure, or large-scale technical systems.

 - Ability to work cross-functionally with engineering, UX design, and customer-facing teams.

 - Experience using data, operational signals, and user feedback to inform product decisions.

 - Strong communication skills and the ability to explain product decisions to diverse stakeholders.

 - Background in networking, telecom, cloud infrastructure, or related domain a plus.

 - Comfort operating in technically complex, ambiguous environments.

 - Ability to work in the Sunnyvale office at least 3 days per week.

It would be great if you also had these:

 - Familiarity with Optical circuit switching

 - Knowledge of Optical networking

 - Knowledge of existing high speed data center inter-connect technologies like Infini Band

The US base salary range for this full-time position is $180,000-$220,000 + bonus + equity + benefits. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your location during the hiring process.

source_jobs.title: Mechanical Design Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About the Team:

Born at X, Google's Moonshot Factory, Taara is on a mission to connect billions of people lacking abundant and affordable internet today by pioneering the way we use light to deliver faster, cheaper, more reliable connectivity. Lead the charge in bringing our groundbreaking wireless optical communication and photonics chip technologies to the world. Drive our growth story as we scale innovative solutions across the world. Join us to light the way for bridging the digital divide and illuminating the future.

About the Role:

Taara is seeking a Mechanical Design Engineer with experience in the design, modeling, fabrication, and validation of mechanical components for the next generation of Wireless Optical Communication (WOC) terminals.

Candidates should have experience in designing complex outdoor products from concept to volume product. The candidate will work closely across teams and support integration of custom silicon photonics, opto-mechanical assemblies and precision steering mechanisms. This is an exciting & dynamic technical leadership position where you will explore, pathfind and define key elements for our next generation WOC architecture options.

The position seeks highly-motivated individuals that enjoy working in a small dynamic team environment, with a passion for solving challenging problems that can lead to high-impact advances in technology.

How you’ll make 10x impact

 - Deliver the mechanical foundation for the next generation wireless optical communication technology by solving challenges in the environmental, materials, and thermal management disciplines.

 - Solve thermo-mechanical system and integrated circuit packaging challenges by providing production solutions

 - Drive reliability and quality testing for Telcordia outdoor equipment including thermal, shock and vibration

 - Provide input to the manufacturing team and partners to ramp up to production quantities.

What you should have:

 - BS degree in Mechanical Engineering, with 4+ years of relevant work experience or a degree in Engineering with relevant 8+ years of experience

 - 3+ years of experience in one of the mainstream 3D mechanical engineering CAD/Simulation software programs (CREO, NX, etc) with proficiency in Solidworks or equivalent

 - 3+ years experience in 3D mechanical engineering design & drawings

 - 1+ years experience with GD&T (Geometric Dimensioning & Tolerancing)

 - 1+ years experience in finite-element analysis to evaluate thermally-induced stresses and structural modes

 - 1+ years experience documenting hardware specifications Proficiency in BOM management and document control

 - Self driven analytical problem solver

 - Strong written and verbal presentation skills, and extreme attention to detail

 - Humility and eagerness to learn

It’d be great if you also had these:

 - Masters in Mechanical Engineering or Physics, with 6+ years of relevant experience

 - Hands-on practical experience with designing agile, light-weight, compact, and high-accuracy pointing mechanism with consideration for DFM

 - Background with MES and Arena or Agile PLM; Tooling and processes for volume production of hardware

 - Experience working with contract manufacturers

 - Familiarity with reliability and test engineering, vibe testing protocols, waterproof testing protocols, various types of seals

 - Proficiency with injection molding plastics and/or die-cast metals

 - Designed or integrated various types of sensors and actuators

 - Proficiency with maker skills, CNC, mill, lathe, soldering, opto-mechanical integration

 - Experience with advanced thermal management systems e.g., metamaterials, thermo-electric, and phase change materials

 - Knowledge of packaging of photonic integrated circuits (PICs)

 - Experience with outdoor product preferred

 - Experience in a startup environment



The US base salary range for this full-time position is $160,000-$210,000 + bonus + equity + benefits. Our salary ranges are det

source_jobs.title: Director Business Development - Data Centers
source_jobs.description:
About the Team:

Born at X, Google's Moonshot Factory, Taara is on a mission to connect billions of people lacking abundant and affordable internet today by pioneering the way we use light to deliver faster, cheaper, more reliable connectivity. Lead the charge in bringing our groundbreaking wireless optical communication and photonics chip technologies to the world. Drive our growth story as we scale innovative solutions across the world. Join us to light the way for bridging the digital divide and illuminating the future.

About the Role:

We are seeking a motivated and experienced Director Business Development, Data Centers to develop strategic partnerships with connectivity providers in the Data Center market. This is an ideal position for an individual who understands the Data Center ecosystem, has existing relationships with key players and is looking to expand their experience to bring new connectivity solutions to this market.

How you will make 10x impact:

 - Define Taara’s Go To Market and business development strategy for entry into the Data Center Market.

 - Identify key players in the Data Center market who provide connectivity solutions to Data Centers.

 - Introduce and establish Taara's FSO technology as an alternative to fiber or as a redundant path/resilience to fiber for connecting Data Centers.

 - Establish strategic partnerships with key players in the Data Center market, to enable adoption and growth of Taara’s FSO technology in the Data Center market

What you should have:

 - Clear understanding of the US Data Center Market ecosystem

 - Established relations with key players in the US Data Center Market

 - At least 3 years of sales/business development experience in the US Data Center Market.

 - 8+ years of experience in new business development, identifying key partners and establishing successful partnerships (preferably in the Telecommunications industry)

 - Proven track record of driving long term sales cycles to closure

 - Experience in understanding new technology and presenting solutions, highlighting value proposition, to key executive contacts across partner organizations

 - Strong oral and written communication skills

 - This is a 100% US remote role

 - Requires 30% to 40% travel in the US

It would be great if you also had these:

 - Salesforce experience is a plus

 - Experience in business development/partnerships in the Data Center Market outside the US

The US base salary range for this full-time position is $250,000 - $300,000+ (inclusive of commission) + equity + benefits.Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your location during the hiring process.

source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Software Engineer, Cloud & Infrastructure
source_jobs.description:
About the Team

Born at X, Google's Moonshot Factory, Taara is on a mission to connect billions of people lacking abundant and affordable internet today by pioneering the way we use light to deliver faster, cheaper, more reliable connectivity. Lead the charge in bringing our groundbreaking wireless optical communication and photonics chip technologies to the world. Drive our growth story as we scale innovative solutions across the world. Join us to light the way for bridging the digital divide and illuminating the future.

About the Role

As a Senior Backend Software Engineer, Cloud & Infrastructure at Taara, you will be the architect of the "brains" behind our global network. While our hardware creates the links, your software manages them. You will design and scale the distributed systems, APIs, and cloud-native infrastructure that monitor, control, and optimize our wireless optical terminals in the field.

The ideal candidate is a generalist who thrives on building reliable, scalable backend systems. You are comfortable jumping between writing high-performance Go services, architecting data pipelines for massive telemetry streams, and automating cloud infrastructure.

How you will make 10x impact:

 - Scale the Control Plane: Design and implement the cloud-native backend responsible for managing thousands of global optical terminals.

 - Architect Telemetry Pipelines: Build robust data ingestion and processing systems to handle real-time performance metrics from the field.

 - Bridge Edge and Cloud: Collaborate with embedded engineers to define how hardware communicates with the cloud, ensuring secure and efficient data flow.

 - Automate Everything: Lead the "Infrastructure as Code" (IaC) strategy to ensure our global deployments are reproducible and resilient.

 - Drive Observability: Develop the tools and dashboards that allow our Network Operations Center (NOC) to troubleshoot complex optical links instantly.

What you should have:

 - Education: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, a related technical field, or equivalent practical experience.

 - Cloud Proficiency: 4+ years of experience building and deploying scalable services on GCP, AWS, or Azure.

 - Core Languages: Strong proficiency in Golang, Typescript, and/or Python.

 - Distributed Systems: Experience with container orchestration (Kubernetes) and distributed systems architecture.

 - API Design: Proven track record of designing and maintaining clean, scalable APIs (gRPC, REST).

 - Data Systems: Experience with relational databases, NoSQL, timeseries databases (e.g., Prometheus), and message brokers (e.g., Pub/Sub, Kafka).

 - Cloud Security: Experience with security best practices, least-privilege model, defining IAM roles/policies, and use of public key cryptography.

 - Location: Ability to work in the Sunnyvale office at least 3 days per week.

It would be great if you also had these:

 - IaC Experience: Familiarity with Terraform, Pulumi, or Ansible.

 - Full-Stack Curiosity: Interest in (or experience with) Angular/React/TypeScript for internal monitoring tools.

 - Networking Knowledge: Understanding of L2/L3 networking, VPNs, and secure tunneling.

 - Startup Mindset: Experience working in fast-paced environments where you might need to wear multiple hats.

 - Hardware Interface: Experience or interest in how cloud services interact with physical IOT or networking hardware.

The US base salary range for this full-time position is $160,000-$210,000 + bonus + equity + benefits. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your location during the hiring process.

source_jobs.title: Systems Software Test Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About the Team

Born at X, Google's Moonshot Factory, Taara is on a mission to connect billions of people lacking abundant and affordable internet today by pioneering the way we use light to deliver faster, cheaper, more reliable connectivity. Join us to ensure the reliability of our groundbreaking wireless optical communication and photonics chip technologies as we scale across the world.

About the Role

Taara is looking for a Systems Software Test Engineer to lead the automation and validation of our end-to-end systems. This isn't just about testing APIs; you will be responsible for building the frameworks that validate how our software controls high-precision hardware in real-time. You will bridge the gap between software development and hardware reliability, ensuring that every code commit improves our network's stability in the field.

How you will make 10x impact:

 - Architect Automated Frameworks: Design and build scalable automation frameworks from the ground up to test embedded software and cloud integrations.

 - Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL): Develop and maintain HIL test benches where real-time software meets physical hardware units, simulating complex field conditions.

 - CI/CD Integration: Own the integration of automated tests into the CI/CD pipeline, ensuring high-quality gates for every build.

 - End-to-End Validation: Work with both the Cloud and Embedded teams to validate the entire data path—from the physical optical link to the backend monitoring dashboard.

 - Regression & Performance: Create automated regression suites that catch performance regressions in network throughput, latency, and system stability.

What you should have:

 - Education: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, a related technical field, or equivalent practical experience.

 - Experience: 4+ years of experience in software testing, QA automation, or system validation.

 - Test Automation: Strong proficiency in Python for test script development and automation.

 - CI/CD Mastery: Hands-on experience with GitHub Actions, Jenkins, or GitLab CI, focusing on automated trigger systems and reporting.

 - Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL): Proven experience testing software on physical devices or using simulation environments (e.g., Raspberry Pis, custom PCBs, or networking gear).

 - Networking Fundamentals: Good understanding of networking protocols (TCP/IP, Ethernet, VLANs) and tools (Wireshark, iPerf).

 - Location: Ability to work in the Sunnyvale office at least 3 days per week.

It would be great if you also had these:

 - Telecom/ISP Background: Experience with network management protocols like gRPC, and SNMP.

 - Physical Layer Testing: Experience with optical sensors, motors, or wireless communication testing.

 - Infrastructure as Code: Experience using Docker/Kubernetes to containerize test environments.

 - Data Analysis: Ability to use SQL or Python (Pandas/NumPy) to analyze large sets of telemetry data from field tests.

 - Creative Breaking: A passion for finding "edge cases" (pun intended) where weather or physical movement might affect software performance.

The US base salary range for this full-time position is $160,000-$210,000 + bonus + equity + benefits. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your location during the hiring process.

source_jobs.title: Senior Hardware Program Manager -  Manufacturing and Quality
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE TEAM

Born at X, Google's Moonshot Factory, Taara is on a mission to connect billions of people lacking abundant and affordable internet today by pioneering the way we use light to deliver faster, cheaper, more reliable connectivity. Lead the charge in bringing our groundbreaking wireless optical communication and photonics chip technologies to the world. Drive our growth story as we scale innovative solutions across the world. Join us to light the way for bridging the digital divide and illuminating the future.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a Manufacturing and Quality Senior Hardware Program Manager at Taara with experience in leading Manufacturing, Manufacturing Engineering and Quality for volume production of Wireless Optical Communication (WOC) systems. These systems are used for delivering abundant and affordable internet.

The role is a strategic, leadership-focused role responsible for overseeing the product build planning, production and shipment and ensuring the product meets the quality and performance specifications and standards by developing, implementing, and enforcing quality control (QC) and quality assurance (QA) protocols throughout the production process.

The position seeks highly-motivated individuals that enjoy working in a small dynamic team environment with engineers, technicians, suppliers and partners and with a passion for solving challenging problems of diverse scope in support of manufacturing, scaling, and continuous improvement projects (CIP) that can lead to high-impact advances in technology.




HOW YOU WILL MAKE 10X IMPACT

 - Production Oversight: Monitor daily manufacturing operations, including production, scheduling, and resource allocation across multiple shifts or value streams. Oversee production capacity, layout, and equipment readiness to ensure manufacturing goals are met. Proactively identify, monitor, and mitigate risks, creating contingency plans to minimize impact on production schedules.

 - Strategy & Planning: Develop and execute long-term manufacturing strategies, including budget management, capacity planning, and capital expenditure proposals. Track and improve Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) such as Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE), yield, and unit costs.

 - Quality Management: Develop and maintain the Quality Management System (QMS), including ISO compliance and, if applicable industry-specific certifications. Create, review, and enforce testing procedures to inspect raw materials, in-process production, and finished goods, passing or rejecting products based on specifications. Evaluate and audit raw material suppliers to ensure components meet quality standards.

 - Data Analysis and Improvement: Drive disposition of non conforming material across manufacturing and supply chain. Monitor quality KPIs, identify trends, analyze root causes of defects, and implement corrective actions (RCCA/CAPA) to improve processes.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with engineering, supply chain, manufacturing and suppliers and lead investigations into quality issues, identifying root causes, and implementing sustainable corrective and preventative actions. Influence product design, manufacturability, testability, and supply chain responsiveness in pursuit of volume production readiness, low cost and flexibility while stewarding quality.

 - Product Compliance: Oversee Process Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (PFMEA), Control Plans, and Production Part Approval Process (PPAP).

 - Budgeting & Reporting: Establish quality budgets and cost commitments, tracking key performance indicators (KPIs) like scrap rate, first-pass yield, and cost per unit.

 - Stakeholder Communication: Present program status, risks, and performance metrics to leadership and key customers.


WHAT YOU SHOULD HAVE

 - Bachelor degree in Mechanical or Electrical or Optics, and 10+ years of experience in product manufacturing, manufacturing engineering and quality in leadership roles.

source_jobs.title: Director of Product Management
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE TEAM

Taara extends and amplifies global communications infrastructure using beams of light to deliver high-speed connectivity. Born at X, Google’s moonshot factory, Taara works with telecommunications providers, partners, and internal teams to plan, deploy, and operate optical wireless networks around the world.

As Taara scales, we are building a new category of connectivity, combining the speed of fiber with the flexibility of wireless.

We are looking for a Director of Product Management to help define this category, drive adoption, and build products that solve real customer problems while scaling revenue.




ABOUT THE ROLE

This role is responsible for shaping and driving a critical part of Taara’s product portfolio. You will own product direction end-to-end, from understanding customer needs to defining the roadmap and delivering capabilities that drive adoption, revenue, and long-term competitive advantage.

You will work closely with engineering, design, sales, and customers to translate complex real-world problems into scalable product solutions.

This role is ideal for someone who is comfortable operating in ambiguity, has strong technical depth, and is excited about building products in a frontier technology space.




WHAT YOU WILL OWN

 - Define and drive the product roadmap aligned with company goals, customer needs, and revenue opportunities

 - Own product outcomes, including adoption, customer satisfaction, and contribution to revenue growth

 - Translate customer needs into clear product requirements, while balancing user experience, technical feasibility, and business impact

 - Partner with engineering to deliver high-quality products on time

 - Work directly with customers to understand real-world use cases and validate product direction

 - Collaborate with sales, marketing, and customer success to enable successful product launches and adoption

 - Drive prioritization decisions across features, bugs, and technical investments

 - Mentor and coach PMs on the team




HOW YOU WILL MAKE 10X IMPACT

 - Build products that unlock new revenue streams and expand Taara’s market

 - Improve user experience and reduce friction across deployment and operations

 - Establish clear product direction in a fast-moving and ambiguous environment

 - Enable Taara to compete effectively with microwave, fiber, and satellite solutions

 - Translate complex telecom and infrastructure challenges into scalable product solutions
   


WHAT YOU SHOULD HAVE

 - 10+ years of product management experience, preferably in telecom, networking, or infrastructure products

 - Strong understanding of networking concepts (e.g., FCAPS, fiber, microwave, Layer 2 switching, on-prem deployments)

 - Experience owning product roadmaps and driving cross-functional execution

 - Ability to translate customer needs into clear product direction and prioritized backlogs

 - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills

 - Excellent communication skills and ability to influence stakeholders

 - BSEE. Preferably an MBA

 - Ability to work in the Sunnyvale office at least 3 days per week.
   
   


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience building products for telecom operators or large infrastructure customers

 - Familiarity with optical systems or wireless backhaul technologies

 - Experience working in startup or high-growth environments

 - Exposure to AI-driven systems or data/analytics products



The US base salary range for this full-time position is $200,000-$250,000 + bonus + equity + benefits. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your location during the hiring process.

source_jobs.title: Growth Marketing Specialist
source_jobs.description:
About the Team:

Born at X, Google's Moonshot Factory, Taara is on a mission to connect billions of people lacking abundant and affordable internet today by pioneering the way we use light to deliver faster, cheaper, more reliable connectivity. Lead the charge in bringing our groundbreaking wireless optical communication and photonics chip technologies to the world. Drive our growth story as we scale innovative solutions across the world. Join us to light the way for bridging the digital divide and illuminating the future.



About the Role:

As a Growth Marketing Specialist at Taara, you will be responsible for transforming complex data into actionable insights that inform Taara’s global marketing and communications strategy and drive global adoption of Taara’s technology in a way that elevates our brand, builds community, and supports the business growth with our partners. You will work closely with cross-functional team members across sales, product, engineering, business development, and operations to optimize the end to end customer journey from lead generation to conversion and growth. This role is ideal for a data-driven strategist who wants to build the analytics foundation of Taara’s marketing tech stack.

This role will be based in Sunnyvale, California.



How you will make 10X impact:

 - Pipeline & Funnel Analytics: Track and analyze the end-to-end customer journey, from awareness and cross-channel engagement to conversion and growth or churn.

 - Growth Marketing: Help maximize lead acquisition, engagement, and retention in partnership with cross-functional teams to optimize hand-offs to and from Marketing and other teams using tools and automated workflows.

 - Insights & Reporting: Build dashboards that unify web traffic, CRM data (Salesforce and HubSpot), and data across marketing tech stack to ensure alignment with revenue and other company growth goals.

 - Marketing Effectiveness: Assess the ROI of marketing campaigns, activations, and digital outreach. Help guide budget allocation based on attribution and lead quality.

 - Field Marketing & Event ROI: Help develop and execute integrated marketing campaigns across digital, content, events, and partner channels to build pipeline, generate qualified leads, and support sales targets, primarily focusing on B2B customer acquisition (ISPs, telcos, resellers). Quantify the impact of activations at local, regional, and global trade shows. Travel as necessary to set up exhibit and physical presence at tradeshows.

 - Data Culture: Champion data transparency, accuracy, and accessibility across Taara. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to refine our marketing data infrastructure to ensure we have a "single source of truth" for growth.



What you should have:

 - 2–4+ years as a marketing data analyst, ideally in a B2B hardware, telecommunications, or deep-tech environment.

 - CRM & Automation: Experience with cross-channel measurement and building automated workflows in Salesforce, HubSpot, and Google Analytics.

 - Testing & Optimization: Hands-on experience with A/B testing web landing pages, social media posts, and email outreach to improve response, engagement, and lead discovery and nurturing.

 - Business Intelligence & Data Visualization: Experience with Tableau, Looker, PowerBI, or equivalent platform to create executive-level reporting and inform cross-functional collaboration

 - Strong Analytical & Modeling Skills: A rigorous approach to data integrity, especially during platform migrations. Data-driven mindset with strong analytical skills and experience using metrics to inform decisions and measure success.

 - Exceptional Communication & Presentation: Proven ability to translate raw data into actionable insights and narrative for technical and non-technical stakeholders.

 - Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or a related field; Master's degree preferred.

It would be great if you also had these:

 - Global Context: Familiar

source_jobs.title: Director of Optical and Mechanical
source_jobs.description:
About the Team:

Born at X, Google's Moonshot Factory, Taara is on a mission to connect billions of people lacking abundant and affordable internet today by pioneering the way we use light to deliver faster, cheaper, and more reliable connectivity. We are a small, dynamic team with a passion for solving challenging problems that lead to high-impact advances in technology. Join us to light the way for bridging the digital divide and illuminating the future.

About the Role:

As our Director of Optical and Mechanical at Taara, you will play a pivotal role in a mission to connect billions of people who lack affordable and abundant internet access today. Your work on our wireless optical communication systems won't just advance technology, it will directly impact lives by providing access to information, education, and economic opportunities.

You will spearhead the development of our advanced wireless optical communication systems from concept to market-ready products. This position centers on leading a multidisciplinary optics, opto-mechanical and mechanical team and driving comprehensive system integration and hardware testing initiatives. Working closely with cross-functional leaders, you will architect cutting-edge wireless optical communication systems that push the boundaries of current technology.

Our Culture & Your Growth:

We operate with the agility and passion of a startup. Our culture is built on first-principles thinking, moving fast, and a supportive environment where you'll work alongside brilliant, mission-driven engineers and industry thought leaders. We have a strong growth culture where we believe in building great people as much as great products.

This role offers a unique opportunity to not only lead a team but also to grow as a technical and strategic leader. You'll be instrumental in developing next-generation technology, and your experience here will give you unparalleled expertise in wireless optical communication and systems integration. You'll have the opportunity to mentor others, shape a team, and build a career at the forefront of a groundbreaking field.

How you will make a 10x impact:

 - Lead and manage the optics, opto-mechanical and mechanical team developing next-generation wireless optical communication technologies

 - Own the optics, opto-mechanical and mechanical design roadmap and drive technical direction across hardware initiatives

 - Provide strong cross-functional leadership with proven team management, execution, and stakeholder influence capabilities

 - Mentor and develop team members to build deep technical expertise and leadership capabilities

 - Drive end-to-end hardware system design, integration, and comprehensive testing protocols

 - Build and expand lab capabilities to support advanced hardware development and validation

 - Develop robust optical test processes and infrastructure to ensure product reliability and performance including test setups emulating environmental channel conditions such as motion, precipitation and atmospheric disturbances

 - Coordinate optical assembly hardware validation testing including thermal, shock, vibration, and environmental stress testing

 - Establish testing methodologies that validate system performance under diverse operational scenarios

What you should have:

 - Education: Master's degree in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Optical Engineering or related technical discipline.

 - Experience: 10+ years of industry experience in hardware engineering with demonstrated progression in technical leadership.

 - Leadership: Proven experience leading multidisciplinary engineering teams in complex hardware development projects.

 - Technical Expertise: Deep technical expertise in optical and mechanical design, modeling and simulation.

 - Ability to work in the Sunnyvale office at least 3 days onsite per week

It’d be great if you also have:

 - Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering, Optical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering o

source_jobs.title: Financial Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
About the Team

Born at X, Google’s Moonshot Factory, Taara is on a mission to connect billions of people lacking abundant and affordable internet today by pioneering the way we use light to deliver faster, cheaper, more reliable connectivity. Lead the charge in bringing our groundbreaking wireless optical communication and photonics chip technologies to the world. Drive our growth story as we scale innovative solutions across the world. Join us to light the way for bridging the digital divide and illuminating the future.

About the Role

Taara is looking for a hands-on Financial Operations Manager to run the day-to-day engine of its finance function. This is a critical role for a venture-backed hardware startup scaling toward its next phase of growth; you will not just be recording transactions, but actively owning how money moves through the company: who we pay, why we pay it, and whether it matches the plan.

From invoice approvals to budget vs actuals, from vendor payments to cash forecasting, and from monthly close to lender reporting, you will serve as the connection point between our operations and our financial statements. You will work hand-in-hand with our accounting partners and our finance leadership, owning the operational cadence and providing the oversight that keeps our books accurate and our reporting on time. You will be responsible for upgrading our processes so that Finance Operations stays ahead of business goals, providing the visibility needed to make the right decisions at speed.



How you will make 10x impact

 - Own the Financial Operations: Manage the end-to-end disbursement of payments to vendors and partners, from purchase order verification through approval and payment. You will pressure-test high-ticket spend with our engineering and operations teams before it goes out the door, establishing a framework that drives financial discipline and accountability.

 - Partner With and Oversee Our Accounting Team: Run the day-to-day relationship with our accounting firm. Review their monthly close output for accuracy, own the customer collections follow-up, and serve as the in-house owner who catches what a remote partner can’t see. You will write the playbook for how Taara’s finance operations run, not just follow it.

 - Run Budget vs Actuals: Build and operate the monthly budget vs actuals cycle. Compile department-level spend reports, run variance reviews with every department lead, and keep forecasts honest as the business changes.

 - Keep Cash and Reporting on Track: Track the weekly cash position, maintain a rolling 13-week cash forecast, and assemble the recurring reporting packages for our lender and investors (GAAP P&L, balance sheet, compliance certificate) so those relationships stay strong and on schedule.

 - Drive Compliance: Own the tax compliance calendar across federal, state, and local obligations, coordinate our external tax firms and our international accounting partner, and drive continuous improvement toward US GAAP compliance, ensuring our books become audit-ready.

 - Build Controls That Scale: Establish, implement, and enforce company spend policies, corporate card programs, and approval workflows with tools that provide real-time data insights, protecting the company without slowing R&D down.

 - Grow Into Controller function and Cost Accounting: As the operational core matures, partner with operations to strengthen our cost accounting practice (inventory reconciliation, landed costs, BOM analysis) for accurate unit economics and reliable financial statements.



What you should have

 - 4-7 years of experience in finance operations or accounting, ideally within venture-backed or mid-size technology, hardware, or manufacturing companies.

 - Hands-on ownership of an end-to-end accounts payable or procure-to-pay cycle, including vendor onboarding, approvals, and payment execution.

 - Experience working alongside accounting providers, or a finance team whose close and books you

source_jobs.title: Principal Analog/Mixed-Signal ASIC Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE TEAM

Born at X, Google's Moonshot Factory, Taara is on a mission to connect billions of people who lack abundant and affordable internet by pioneering high-throughput, long-range free-space optical communication (FSOC). Our links use eye-safe lasers propagating between two terminals to transmit high-speed data wirelessly. Lead the charge in bringing our groundbreaking wireless optical communication to the world. Join us to light the way toward bridging the digital divide and illuminating the future.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Taara is seeking a Principal Analog/Mixed-Signal ASIC Engineer to work on the next generation of co-integrated electronic and photonic modules. You will be part of an R&D team developing cutting-edge wireless optical communication systems based on integrated optical phased arrays.

The successful candidate will lead ASIC tape-out efforts, own the ASIC top-level architecture, and drive the delivery and validation of ASIC dies. This includes making hands-on contributions to Analog/Mixed-Signal (AMS) ASIC designs and coordinating a small team of ASIC design and layout engineers. The role requires extensive experience in ASIC design and layout—including top-level ownership of ASIC tape-outs at leading commercial foundries—and deep expertise in either or both of these two categories: high-precision AMS circuits and high-speed wireline/silicon photonics interface circuits.

We are looking for a highly motivated individual who thrives in ambiguity, enjoys working in a small, dynamic team environment, and has a passion for solving challenging problems that lead to high-impact technological advances.




HOW YOU WILL MAKE A 10X IMPACT

 - Own and deliver custom ASICs for co-integration with large-scale photonic integrated circuits to form record-breaking integrated modules.

 - Contribute to ASIC design and layout, system-level optimization, and product strategy.

 - Demonstrate a strong motivation to make the world a better place through technology and a desire to be part of the team making this happen.


WHAT YOU SHOULD HAVE

 - Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering or a related field.

 - 10 or more years of relevant industry experience with demonstrated achievements in analog/mixed-signal circuit design and implementation in modern CMOS technologies.

 - Demonstrated deep design expertise in one or both of the following categories:
   
   - High-precision designs: Analog-to-digital and digital-to-analog converters, bandgap references, voltage regulators, amplifiers, and other supporting circuitry. Integration and co-simulation of analog and digital circuits in the context of mixed-signal design and verification.
   
   - High-speed designs: Front-end circuits for optical communication, including modulator drivers and transimpedance amplifiers (TIAs) operating at tens of GHz, and broadband circuits for serial links including receivers, transmitters, and equalizers. Electromagnetic design of high-frequency transmission lines, inductors, and other passive elements.

 - Experience owning top-level ASIC architecture and coordinating engineering teams.

 - Hands-on experience leading multiple ASIC tape-outs at commercial foundries.

 - Hands-on experience with Design Rule Checks (DRC), Layout Versus Schematic (LVS), Power Integrity (PI), and Signal Integrity (SI) verification.


IT WOULD BE GREAT IF YOU ALSO HAD

 - Experience with planning and executing verification of large Systems-on-Chip (SoCs) incorporating complex digital logic and large numbers of custom-designed blocks.

 - Experience with hardware description languages, such as Register-Transfer Level (RTL) code, and RTL-to-GDS design flows using industry-standard EDA tools for synthesis, timing analysis, and place-and-route.

 - Experience with versioning tools such as SOS and Git.

 - Experience with commercial layout verification software (Calibre or equivalent).

 - Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to work effectively with

notes: High-tech startup with strong engineering focus, pioneering wireless optical communication and photonics chip technologies.
